action.reasoning: Observation: train.py (and likely any import of SchNet) crashes with SIGSEGV at import time. Exit code 139 indicates segmentation fault. Analysis: The PyG SchNet or its dependency (torch_scatter) has a binary incompatibility with the CUDA runtime or driver in this environment. Since SchNet from torch_geometric.nn is implemented using scatter operations, this is a known issue. TorchMD-Net models (PaiNN, TensorNet) might not suffer from the same import crash. Decision: Before replacing the model, run a diagnostic command to see exactly which import segfaults. This will inform whether we can use another PyG model or must switch to TorchMD-Net entirely.
action.command: python -u -c "import sys; print('start', flush=True); import torch; print('torch ok', flush=True); import torch_geometric; print('pyg ok', flush=True); from torch_geometric.nn import SchNet; print('schnet ok', flush=True); print('success', flush=True)"
